reviewTarget: Audit related skill documentation (SKILL.md/references/assets) and README files across the repo for stale /goal OpenCode plugin behavior after phases 010-014 remediation + goal_opencode.md filename correction; independently AUDIT the 6 companion research findings with P0/P1/P2 verdicts and search for anything missed

findingDetails.title: Central env reference omits active goal-state cleanup controls
findingDetails.evidence: mk-goal.js defines MK_GOAL_STATE_ARCHIVE_RETENTION_DAYS, MK_GOAL_STATE_ACTIVE_RETENTION_DAYS, and MK_GOAL_STATE_SWEEP_INTERVAL_MS at lines 40-42, uses them in archive/sweep retention paths at lines 825-837 and 874-883, but ENV_REFERENCE.md goal-plugin table at lines 646-660 lists only older controls.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add rows for all three MK_GOAL_STATE_* variables to the OPENCODE GOAL PLUGIN table with defaults 90 days, 30 days, and 1 hour plus source references.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Source-side env definitions and consumers verified in mk-goal.js; central env table verified in ENV_REFERENCE.md; scoped inventory built for follow-up doc classes.

claimAdjudication.claim: ENV_REFERENCE.md omits the three MK_GOAL_STATE_* cleanup/archive env vars that mk-goal.js defines and consumes.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Read the OPENCODE GOAL PLUGIN table and searched scoped skill docs for the same env vars; no ENV_REFERENCE.md rows for the three variables were found.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The table links to detailed operator guidance, but ENV_REFERENCE.md is the central env reference and already owns the goal-plugin env table.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade/refute if ENV_REFERENCE.md documents all three variables elsewhere in an authoritative goal-plugin env table, or if mk-goal.js stops reading those variables.

noveltyJustification: First review iteration confirmed one new active P1 against live source and central env reference evidence.
ruledOut: generic goal mentions in sk-prompt and deep-improvement asset are not plugin-specific
edgeCases: memory_match_triggers rejected the external review session id, so packet sessionId was preserved only in JSONL and memory lookup was retried without session scope

findingsNew.title: Root README delegates the /goal plugin contract to an entrypoint inventory that does not define that contract
findingsNew.claim: The public root README sends operators to .opencode/plugins/README.md for the /goal plugin contract, but that document does not contain the mk-goal config/contract surface needed for the state, injection, lifecycle and autonomy claims the root README is describing.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether .opencode/plugins/README.md had a mk-goal config/contract subsection, a link to the goal contract, or goal env/output/archive behavior beyond the entrypoint row; none was present in the reviewed ranges.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: If the root README intended only the low-level OpenCode loader/export contract, .opencode/plugins/README.md:26-28 and .opencode/plugins/README.md:49 partially satisfy that. The surrounding root bullets discuss /goal state, injection and autonomy, so the operator-facing goal contract is the more likely intended target.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the root README wording is narrowed to entrypoint loader contract or if .opencode/plugins/README.md gains a first-class mk-goal contract subsection linked from the root pointer.

findingsNew.title: Skill Advisor README contradicts the feature catalog's verified /goal live-tool status
findingsNew.claim: The system-skill-advisor README says /goal live OpenCode-run tool invocation remains under investigation, while the sibling feature catalog says a live opencode serve run verified tool listing and live model state persistence.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked the feature catalog for whether it only documented unit/tool-path tests or an unverified plan; it explicitly claims opencode serve tool listing plus a live model turn persisting state.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The README may have intended to say active autonomous continuation invocation is still under investigation, not basic mk_goal / mk_goal_status tool invocation. If so, the wording is ambiguous and should be narrowed.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to a no-finding wording cleanup if the README is clarified to refer only to live autonomous continuation and the feature catalog keeps basic tool invocation verification separate.

findingDetails.title: Root README delegates the /goal plugin contract to an entrypoint inventory that does not define that contract
findingDetails.scopeProof: Cross-read the public root pointer, the plugin entrypoint document and the dedicated goal contract reference; this affects root docs and the plugin/operator docs rather than an isolated typo.

findingDetails.title: Skill Advisor README contradicts the feature catalog's verified /goal live-tool status
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact sibling-doc contradiction found between one README line and one feature catalog line; no broader same-class claim was made.

priorFindings: P1-002 indirectly strengthened but not duplicated

findingsNew.title: Manual playbooks can pass without checking new goal status surfaces
findingsNew.claim: Both goal-plugin manual testing playbooks can pass without explicitly verifying store_health= output or /goal set mutation=<created|refreshed|replaced> output.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Exact search for store_health, mutation=, and goal.md across goal-opencode-plugin.md files returned no matches. Semantic search found only a generic post-mutation-state check in the system-skill-advisor playbook, which does not require the literal mutation enum or store health output.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The generic post-mutation-state instruction could indirectly catch some response regressions if an operator already knows the mutation enum exists, but it does not force coverage of created/refreshed/replaced or store_health=.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade or close if either playbook is amended to require store_health= and mutation=<created|refreshed|replaced> checks, or if an existing referenced test transcript is shown to assert those exact fields as a mandatory pass criterion.
findingsNew.scopeProof: Both target playbooks were read in full; exact and semantic sweeps were run across goal-opencode-plugin.md files under .opencode/skills.
findingsNew.recommendation: Add explicit pass criteria for store_health= and /goal set mutation=<created|refreshed|replaced>, including refreshed/replaced where practical.

findingDetails.title: Manual playbooks can pass without checking new goal status surfaces
findingDetails.evidence: System Spec Kit playbook expected signals cover STATUS=OK, goal_prompt metadata, prompt metadata, and injection preview; System Skill Advisor playbook adds a generic post-mutation-state check but neither playbook names store_health= or mutation=<created|refreshed|replaced> as a pass criterion.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add explicit manual validation steps for store_health= and the mutation enum output.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Read both playbooks in full and ran exact plus semantic searches. No literal store_health or mutation= coverage exists; only system-skill-advisor has partial generic mutation-response coverage.

findingDetails.title: Packet-history docs still contain current stale goal.md operational claims
findingDetails.claim: A small number of packet-history docs still present .opencode/commands/goal.md as a current operational target or assert stale-reference cleanliness that is no longer literally true.
counterEvidence: .opencode/specs/deep-loops/032-goal-opencode-plugin/011-command-surface-normalization/implementation-summary.md:117 explicitly classifies remaining goal.md hits as historical narrative
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/constitutional/goal-prompting-runtime-specific.md:25-50 names goal_opencode.md as current and keeps past names as rename history
findingDetails.scopeProof: Glob .opencode/commands/*goal*.md returned only .opencode/commands/goal_opencode.md; narrowed rg excluding review/research/changelog/archive folders found active hits only in phase 009 handover and phase 011 docs plus explicit historical rename narrative.
findingDetails.recommendation: Annotate or leave per operator preference; if edited later, update only current operational lines and preserve historical narrative.

findingDetails.title: Goal-plugin docs lack a single contract owner and canonical output-field schema
findingDetails.claim: The documentation topology itself makes future goal-plugin contract changes drift-prone because env vars are documented in multiple places while output fields have no canonical schema section.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: A single authoritative mk-goal operator-contract doc listing both env vars and status/output fields; none was found in the reviewed docs.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: This could be a one-off propagation miss by the implementer rather than a topology problem, but the same code change drifted across env docs, output docs, delegation docs, and playbooks, while output fields have no canonical section.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to note/no-finding if goal_plugin.md gains a dedicated status-output schema and README/plugin README delegation is corrected to point to it.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Read coverage included ENV_REFERENCE.md, goal_plugin.md, root README, .opencode/plugins/README.md, feature catalog, two playbooks, and mk-goal.js output renderer.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make goal_plugin.md the canonical operator contract, add an Output Fields table aligned with goalStateLines, and convert other docs to pointers or validation-only references.

traceabilityChecks.codeSelfDocumentation: adequate; output rendering centralized in goalStateLines

findingDetails.title: Central env reference carries a stale generated-date marker while missing shipped goal-state env vars
findingDetails.claim: ENV_REFERENCE.md says it was generated from source analysis and last updated on 2026-06-10, but its goal-plugin env table omits state cleanup env vars shipped and documented on 2026-07-01.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched env examples, JSON/JSONC/YAML config and manifest surfaces, and skill markdown MK_GOAL_* mentions for another current generated env reference or inline mk-goal env table; none was found beyond the already-open ENV_REFERENCE and goal_plugin gaps.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The date may represent the last full generator run rather than hand edits, but the wording still tells readers the table came from source analysis and is stale relative to current source.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade or close if the marker is removed, regenerated, or explicitly scoped as historical/non-authoritative while the missing env rows are added.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update or regenerate the generated/last-updated marker as part of the P1-001 env-table fix.

traceabilityChecks.spec_code: pass: re-read mk-goal env definitions and output fields against docs

traceabilityChecks.config_manifest: pass: opencode.json and JSON/JSONC/YAML searches found no inline mk-goal env docs
traceabilityChecks.changelog_coverage: partial: phase summaries disclose fields, packet changelog stops at phase 008

findingDetails.title: Registered tool-path tests do not pin mutation=refreshed or mutation=replaced
findingDetails.claim: The implementation emits three /goal set mutation states, but registered tool-path tests only assert mutation=created, leaving refreshed/replaced output branches unpinned.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: State-level tests cover same-objective behavior and lifecycle tests cover retention/sweep behavior; this is an output regression-test gap, not evidence of an implementation defect.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Close if registered tool-path tests assert both mutation=refreshed and mutation=replaced, or if docs intentionally scope the output contract to mutation=created only.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Source selects created/refreshed/replaced at .opencode/plugins/mk-goal.js:1668-1675; direct test grep found only mutation=created coverage.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add focused registered tool-path assertions for same-objective set -> mutation=refreshed and changed-objective set -> mutation=replaced.

traceabilityChecks.spec_code: partial-pass: source output contract verified; mutation output tests incomplete
